description:
Add two new check functions to `wai way` (`src/commands/way.rs`):

## check_beads(repo_root)
- **Name**: "Issue tracking"
- **Intent**: Structured task tracking keeps work visible and prevents context loss across sessions.
- **Success criteria**: A beads workspace (.beads/) exists for tracking issues and dependencies.
- Detect `.beads/` directory in repo root (`.exists() && .is_dir()`) → Status::Pass, message "beads detected"
  - Optional enhancement: if `.beads/issues.jsonl` is readable, surface issue count: "beads detected (N issues tracked)"
- If `.beads/` missing → Status::Info, suggestion: "Initialize beads issue tracking — https://github.com/steveyegge/beads"
  - If suggesting a command: `bd init` is the correct initialization command

## check_openspec(repo_root)
- **Name**: "Change proposals"
- **Intent**: Formal change proposals prevent architectural drift and create a reviewable design record.
- **Success criteria**: An openspec workspace (openspec/) exists for managing change proposals.
- Detect `openspec/` directory in repo root (`.exists() && .is_dir()`) → Status::Pass, message "openspec detected"
- If missing → Status::Info, suggestion: "Track architectural change proposals — https://github.com/Fission-AI/OpenSpec"
  - If suggesting a command: `openspec init` is the correct initialization command

## Integration
Add both checks to the `checks` vec in `run()` in `src/commands/way.rs`, alongside existing checks.

## Notes
- Follow the same CheckResult pattern as all other check_* functions in way.rs
- Always pair `.exists()` with `.is_dir()` for directory checks — matches pattern at e.g. way.rs check_devcontainer and check_ci_cd
- Check repo_root directly (no recursive search)
- No external process calls needed — pure filesystem checks
- Add tests in `tests/integration.rs` covering: beads present → Pass, beads absent → Info, same for openspec. Model on existing way check tests.
title: feat(way): add beads and openspec checks

description:
Tool work: implement the `wai ls` top-level CLI command per openspec/changes/add-ls-command/. Requires approved openspec before merging.

## What
New `wai ls` command that scans for wai workspaces under a root directory (default: $HOME, depth: 3) and shows each project's phase and open issue count as a one-line table.

Expected output:
```
why-command   [review]    3 open, 2 ready
para          [plan]      7 open, 0 ready
rizomas       [implement] 1 open, 1 ready
```

## Key design decisions (see openspec/changes/add-ls-command/design.md)
- Traversal: use walkdir with follow_links(false); check for .wai/config.toml per directory; skip hidden dirs; do NOT descend into .wai/ itself
- Counts column: global toggle — shown for ALL rows when any workspace has beads, blank cell for non-beads rows, omitted entirely when no workspace has beads; counts are per-workspace (one bd invocation per workspace), shared across all project rows from that workspace
- Duplicate names: append path suffix when two projects share a name: `name (~/path/to/repo)`
- dirs and walkdir crates confirmed in Cargo.toml

## Tasks (from openspec/changes/add-ls-command/tasks.md)

### 1. CLI definition
- [ ] 1.1 Add `Ls { root: Option<PathBuf>, depth: Option<usize> }` to `Commands` enum in `src/cli.rs`
- [ ] 1.2 Add `"ls"` to `valid_commands` array in `run_external()` in `src/commands/mod.rs`

### 2. Workspace discovery
- [ ] 2.1 Add `discover_workspaces(root, max_depth) -> Vec<PathBuf>` using walkdir with follow_links(false); for each directory check if .wai/config.toml exists within it (match = wai workspace); do NOT recurse into .wai/ itself; skip all other hidden directories during traversal
- [ ] 2.2 For each workspace, enumerate project names by listing subdirectories of `.wai/projects/` via `std::fs::read_dir()` — use the `projects_dir()` helper from `src/config.rs`. IMPORTANT: `.wai/config.toml` holds only the workspace name (`[project] name`), NOT a list of projects; do not try to read project names from config.toml
- [ ] 2.3 For each project, read phase using `ProjectState::load()` from `src/state.rs` (reads `.wai/projects/<project>/.state` as YAML, `current` field); default to `unknown` on missing file or parse error (ProjectState::load already handles missing files gracefully)

### 3. Plugin integration (beads, when detected)
- [ ] 3.1 If `.beads/` exists in the workspace directory, invoke `bd stats --json` to get `open` and `ready` counts; skip gracefully if `bd` is not installed or exits non-zero
- [ ] 3.2 Store counts at workspace level; apply the same open/ready counts to all project rows from that workspace when rendering (bd is invoked once per workspace, not once per project — all projects in a beads-enabled workspace share the same counts)

### 4. Command implementation
- [ ] 4.1 Create `src/commands/ls.rs`
- [ ] 4.2 Resolve root: use `--root <path>` if provided (fail with diagnostic if path does not exist), else `$HOME` via `dirs::home_dir()`; fail with diagnostic if home directory cannot be determined
- [ ] 4.3 Resolve depth: use `--depth <n>` if provided, else default to 3
- [ ] 4.4 Run workspace discovery, collect results sorted by project name
- [ ] 4.5 Render table: one line per (workspace, project) pair with columns left-aligned and padded to longest name; show counts column for ALL rows when any workspace has beads data (blank cell for non-beads rows); omit counts column entirely when no workspace has beads; append `(~/path)` suffix to disambiguate duplicate project names
- [ ] 4.6 Print `No wai workspaces found under <root>` when discovery returns nothing

### 5. Wire up
- [ ] 5.1 Dispatch `Commands::Ls` in `src/commands/mod.rs`
- [ ] 5.2 Verify `wai ls --help` output describes the command and references `--root` and `--depth`

### 6. Tests
- [ ] 6.1 Integration test: root with one workspace and one project → one-line output with correct phase
- [ ] 6.2 Integration test: root with multiple workspaces → all projects shown, sorted by name
- [ ] 6.3 Integration test: no workspaces found under root → empty-output message
- [ ] 6.4 Integration test: workspace with beads detected → counts column shown for all rows; blank cell for non-beads rows
- [ ] 6.5 Integration test: no workspace has beads → counts column omitted entirely
- [ ] 6.6 Integration test: `--depth 1` stops recursion at the expected level
- [ ] 6.7 Integration test: `--root /nonexistent` → diagnostic error with the invalid path

## References
- Openspec: openspec/changes/add-ls-command/
- Design: openspec/changes/add-ls-command/design.md
- Data model: src/config.rs (projects_dir, ProjectConfig), src/state.rs (ProjectState)
- Reference implementation: src/commands/status.rs (project enumeration pattern at line ~30)
- Design ticket closed: wai-ycb

Content to add (verbatim from openspec/changes/add-resume-loop/design.md § Autonomous Loop Content):

### Autonomous Loop

One task per session. The resume loop:

1. `wai prime` — orient (shows ⚡ RESUMING if mid-task)
2. Work on the single task
3. `wai close` — capture state (run this before every `/clear`)
4. `git add <files> && git commit`
5. `/clear` — fresh context

→ Next session: `wai prime` shows RESUMING with exact next steps.

When context reaches ~40%: run `wai close`, then `/clear`.
Do NOT skip `wai close` — it enables resume detection.

Spec: openspec/changes/add-resume-loop/

description:
Add an 11th check to `wai way` that detects whether the repo has a release pipeline configured.

## Check logic

- Look for `.goreleaser.yml` (Go, Rust) or `dist.toml` / `[workspace.metadata.dist]` in `Cargo.toml` (cargo-dist)
- Also accept `.github/workflows/release.yml` (or any workflow with `tags: ["v*"]`) as sufficient
- Only flag if there is also at least one binary target (binary in Cargo.toml [[bin]], Go main package, `[project.scripts]` in pyproject.toml, etc.) — pure library repos should not be flagged

## Recommendation message

When missing:
  'No release pipeline found. Consider goreleaser (Go/Rust/any) or cargo-dist (Rust) to automate GitHub releases and publish to Homebrew/Scoop.'

When present: show green tick with the detected tool name (e.g. 'goreleaser', 'cargo-dist').

## Fix behaviour

No --fix for this check (release pipeline setup is non-trivial to auto-scaffold).

description:
Building with `just install` produces 19 compiler warnings (13 lib + 6 bin, with some overlap). Categories:

**Unused imports (3):**

- `std::path::Path` in src/commands/sync.rs:5

**Unused struct field assignments in error.rs (10):**
These are 'value assigned to X is never read' warnings on thiserror enum fields. Likely the fields are declared but not referenced in the `#[error(...)]` format string. Fix by either using them in the Display impl or removing them.
- `path` in ProjectExists (line 28)
- `name` in ProjectNotFound (line 35)
- `name` in AreaNotFound (line 42)
- `name` in ResourceNotFound (line 49)
- `to` in move variant (line 58)
- `message` in ConfigSyncError (line 74)
- `message` in HandoffError (line 78)
- `name` in PluginNotFound (line 85)
- `message` in NonInteractive (line 92)
- `action` in SafeModeViolation (line 99)

**Unused variables (1):**
- `content` in src/guided_flows.rs:28 (assigned from input but never used)

**Dead code (3):**
- `is_first_run` in src/config.rs:222
- `run_non_interactive` in src/tutorial.rs:96
- `should_show_tutorial` in src/tutorial.rs:117

description:
Tool work: implement the `wai close` top-level CLI command per openspec/changes/add-close-command/. Requires approved openspec before merging.

## What
New `wai close` command that automates the session-end checklist: create a handoff for the active project, surface uncommitted files, and suggest the next steps.

Expected output (the `!` line uses comma-separated bare names; quotes appear only in the `git add` suggestion):
```
✓ Handoff created: .wai/projects/<project>/handoffs/2026-02-24-session.md
! Uncommitted changes: src/main.rs, CLAUDE.md
→ Next: bd sync --from-main && git add "src/main.rs" "CLAUDE.md" && git commit
```

## Tasks (from openspec/changes/add-close-command/tasks.md)

### 1. CLI definition
- [ ] 1.1 Add `Close { project: Option<String> }` variant to `Commands` enum in `src/cli.rs`

### 2. Command implementation
- [ ] 2.1 Create `src/commands/close.rs`; note: `close.rs` formats its own `✓ Handoff created: <full-path>` success line — do NOT reuse the existing log message from `handoff.rs` (different wording)
- [ ] 2.2 Auto-detect project: fail with "no projects" diagnostic if none exist; use the single project if only one exists; prompt (cliclack select) if multiple; fail with diagnostic when `--no-input` and multiple projects
- [ ] 2.3 Extract `create_handoff(project_root: &Path, project: &str) -> Result<PathBuf>` from `src/commands/handoff.rs`; this function contains ONLY the pure handoff-creation logic — `require_safe_mode("create handoff")` stays at each call site (both `wai handoff create` and `close.rs` call it before invoking the extracted function); both `wai handoff create` and `wai close` call the extracted function
- [ ] 2.4 Read git status: shell out to `git status --porcelain`; treat non-zero exit (git not installed, not a git repo) as "no git" and skip the git section silently
- [ ] 2.5 Render uncommitted files as a single `!`-prefixed line with comma-separated bare filenames; cap at 10 files and append `… and N more` when exceeded
- [ ] 2.6 Print plugin-aware next-steps: include `bd sync --from-main &&` prefix only when beads plugin is detected; include the uncommitted filenames in the `git add` suggestion, each wrapped in double-quotes to handle paths with spaces

### 3. Wire up
- [ ] 3.1 Dispatch `Commands::Close` in `src/commands/mod.rs`
- [ ] 3.2 Add `"close"` to `valid_commands` in the external subcommand handler
- [ ] 3.3 Verify `wai close --help` output matches expected format and references `--project`

### 4. Tests
- [ ] 4.1 Integration test: single project workspace → handoff created, output matches expected format (with beads plugin detected: assert `bd sync --from-main` present in next-steps)
- [ ] 4.2 Integration test: `--project <name>` flag creates handoff without interactive prompt
- [ ] 4.3 Integration test: unknown project name → diagnostic error lists available projects
- [ ] 4.4 Integration test: zero projects workspace → diagnostic error suggests `wai new project`
- [ ] 4.5 Integration test: repeated invocation on same day → second handoff uses incremented suffix (e.g. session-end-1.md), first file not overwritten or errored

## References
- Openspec: openspec/changes/add-close-command/
- Design ticket closed: wai-t86

description:
Bulk-import existing skills into `.wai/resources/agent-config/skills/`.

## Behaviour
1. Default source: `.agents/skills/` in workspace root
2. Optional `--from <path>` to specify alternate source
3. For each `<name>/SKILL.md` found in source:
   a. Copy entire `<name>/` directory (SKILL.md, scripts/, toolbox/, mcp.json)
   b. Skip if target already exists (warn per-skill)
4. Report: N imported, N skipped
5. Remind to run `wai sync`
6. Respect `--safe` mode

## Acceptance Criteria
- Copies full directory trees (not just SKILL.md)
- Skips existing with warning, doesn't overwrite
- Works with both .agents/skills/ and custom paths
- Handles source path not existing (clear error)
- Safe mode respected

description: Before any wai command can read or write bd memories, establish how wai interacts with the bd store. Options: (1) shell out to 'bd memories <query>' / 'bd remember <text>' CLI commands — simple but couples wai to bd binary being on PATH; (2) query Dolt directly via SQL on the known port — faster but tight coupling to bd internals; (3) plugin API — wai detects bd as a plugin and calls a defined interface. Decision should consider: agent/CI contexts where bd may not be on PATH, error handling when bd is unavailable (wai should degrade gracefully, not fail), and whether the mechanism is the same for read (memories) and write (remember). Output: a wai research artifact documenting the chosen approach, referenced by all bd-memory integration tickets.
notes: Decision: shell out to bd CLI via the existing execute_hook pattern in src/plugin.rs. Add fetch_memories(project_root) -> Option<String> (runs 'bd memories') and store_memory(project_root, text) -> Result<()> (runs 'bd remember') as helpers in src/plugin.rs. Graceful degradation: None/Err = bd unavailable, skip silently. MEMORIES_BUDGET = 10_000 chars. Research artifact saved to .wai.
title: Define bd memory integration mechanism for wai

description: WhyConfig has api_key: Option<String> serialized to .wai/config.toml. This means the key gets committed to git and potentially pushed to a public repo. description:
Enhance `check_agent_config_sync()` in doctor.rs to detect content inconsistencies between source and projected targets.

## Current State
Only checks: .projections.yml parses + target paths exist.

## New Checks

### All strategies
- Warn if source directory in projections doesn't exist

### Symlink strategy
- Verify each entry in target is a symlink pointing to correct source path
- Detect broken symlinks

### Inline strategy
- Rebuild expected concatenated content in memory
- SHA-256 hash comparison against target file
- No mtime dependency (unreliable across git ops)

### Reference strategy
- Rebuild expected reference list, compare to target content

## Output
- Pass: "Projection → <target> is in sync"
- Warn: "Projection → <target> is stale (N files changed)" + fix: "Run: wai sync"
- Warn: "Projection → <target> has broken symlinks" + fix: "Run: wai sync"
- Warn: "Projection source '<dir>' not found" + fix: "Check .projections.yml sources"

## Implementation
- Add `sources: Vec<String>` to doctor's local `ProjectionEntry` struct
- Reuse inline/reference content building logic from sync.rs (or extract shared helper)

description:
Implement error handling, graceful degradation, and fallback behavior for wai why.

## Tasks

- [ ] 5.1 Graceful degradation to wai search if no LLM available
- [ ] 5.2 Handle LLM API errors with miette diagnostics (invalid_api_key, rate_limit, network_error, model_not_found)
- [ ] 5.3 Detect Ollama model not downloaded and suggest ollama pull
- [ ] 5.4 Handle rate limits with clear message (wait 60s or use Ollama)
- [ ] 5.5 Add --no-llm flag to force fallback for testing

## Architecture

Fallback flow: if LLM unavailable → eprintln warning + call wai_search(query). If LLM errors mid-request → eprintln warning + call wai_search(query). --no-llm flag bypasses LLM entirely (for CI, privacy, testing).

UX message patterns (miette diagnostics, owo_colors):
- No LLM: warning + remediation (install Ollama or set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
- No artifacts: warning + suggest wai add
- Git unavailable: informational
- Rate limited: clear wait message
- Ollama model missing: suggest ollama pull <model>

## References

- Design: openspec/changes/add-why-command/design.md (Decision 4: Graceful Degradation, Risks 1-4)
- Tasks: openspec/changes/add-why-command/tasks.md

description:
## Problem

`wai way` checks for prek installation by looking for the string 'prek' in `.git/hooks/pre-commit`. This produces false negatives — and misleading suggestions — in repos where another hook manager has set `core.hooksPath` or displaced prek's hook files.

## Verified Evidence (this repo, 2026-03-01)

- `prek.toml` exists with pre-commit and pre-push hooks defined
- `git config core.hooksPath` = `.git/hooks` (set by bd)
- Running `prek install` errors immediately: 'Cowardly refusing to install hooks with `core.hooksPath` set' — prek does NOT silently fail or overwrite; it refuses entirely
- `.git/hooks/pre-commit` is a bd shim (`bd hooks run pre-commit`)
- `.git/hooks/pre-push` is a bd shim (`bd hooks run pre-push`)
- `wai way` reports: "prek.toml found but hooks not installed — run: prek install"
- That suggestion is wrong: running it would fail with a git config error, not install anything

## Root Causes

1. **`core.hooksPath` not checked**: prek refuses to install when this is set; `wai way` never inspects git config
2. **Only pre-commit checked**: prek installs pre-push hooks too; detection only reads pre-commit
3. **Hook owner not identified**: when another tool's shim is present, the message should name it rather than suggest a re-install
4. **All managed-hook sigils not covered**: way.rs detects 'bd', 'lefthook', 'husky', 'pre-commit' as owners elsewhere; prek branch should use the same patterns

## What needs to change in `src/commands/way.rs` — `check_git_hooks()` (~line 289)

1. **Check `core.hooksPath` git config**: if set to anything other than the default, prek cannot install — report this specifically with the hint prek itself gives (unset local + global)
2. **Check pre-push hook unconditionally** (not stage-aware): always inspect both pre-commit and pre-push for prek signature — simpler than parsing prek.toml stages
3. **Detect hook owner on mismatch**: when prek.toml exists but hook is owned by another tool (contains 'bd', 'lefthook', 'husky', 'pre-commit'), name the conflicting tool in the message
4. **Binary check is separate**: if `prek` is not on PATH, report that distinctly — it is an enhancement, not part of the conflict-detection fix
5. **Suggestion precision**: replace generic 'run: prek install' with context-aware hint (e.g., 'unset core.hooksPath first' or 'hook owned by bd — chain prek or use bd's hook runner')

## Acceptance Criteria

- Repo with prek.toml + `core.hooksPath` set → reports git config conflict with unset hint, not 'run prek install'
- Repo with prek.toml + bd/lefthook/husky shim in hook file → reports which tool owns the hook, not generic 'not installed'
- Repo with prek.toml + no hooks at all + prek on PATH → suggests `prek install` (existing behavior preserved)
- Repo with prek properly installed (hook contains 'prek', no `core.hooksPath` conflict) → passes (existing behavior preserved)
- Repo with no prek.toml → check does not fire at all
- Repo where bd hook internally delegates to prek → if hook file contains 'prek', still reports pass

## Test Strategy

Add unit tests in `src/commands/way.rs` for `check_git_hooks()` using a temp-dir fixture (pattern established in existing tests). Cover each acceptance criterion scenario.
title: Fix wai way prek detection: check core.hooksPath, all hook stages, and tool conflicts

description:
## Problem

`wai way` currently checks for the *presence of a hook manager config file*
(prek.toml, lefthook.yml, etc.) but not whether the hooks are actually
*installed* into `.git/hooks/`. This gives a false ✓ — exactly what bit us:
`lefthook.yml` existed for weeks but `lefthook install` was never run, so
fmt/lint gates silently never fired on any commit.

## Proposed Fix

Enhance `check_git_hooks` in `src/commands/way.rs` to do a two-level check:

**Level 1 — config present** (current behaviour)
**Level 2 — hooks installed** (new)

When a config file is detected, also inspect `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (and
`.git/hooks/pre-push` where relevant) to confirm the hook manager's shim is
actually installed. If the config exists but the hook is missing or is not
managed by the detected tool, downgrade the result to `Status::Info` with an
actionable suggestion.

### Detection logic per tool

| Tool | Config file | Installed signal |
|------|-------------|------------------|
| prek | `prek.toml` | `.git/hooks/pre-commit` contains `prek` |
| lefthook | `lefthook.yml` | `.git/hooks/pre-commit` contains `lefthook` |
| husky | `.husky/` | `.git/hooks/pre-commit` exists and is non-sample |
| pre-commit | `.pre-commit-config.yaml` | `.git/hooks/pre-commit` contains `pre-commit` |

### Suggested messages

- Config present + hooks installed → `"prek detected and installed"` ✓
- Config present + hooks **not** installed → `"prek.toml found but hooks not installed — run: prek install"` ℹ
- No config at all → existing message

## Scope

- Change is in `src/commands/way.rs` → `check_git_hooks()`
- Add/update integration tests in `tests/integration.rs` for the
  "config present but not installed" case (create a `.git/hooks/` dir
  without a prek shim, assert the result is `Status::Info`)
- No changes needed to `wai doctor` (that command is wai-workspace-specific;
  this is a general repo check)
title: wai way: verify git hooks are installed, not just configured

description:
The version check in `wai doctor` relied on a manually-bumped CalVer string in Cargo.toml, which meant the check silently passed when the binary changed but the version wasn't bumped.

## Changes

- **config.rs**: Added `tool_commit: String` field to `ProjectSettings` (skipped in serialization when empty for backwards compat with old workspaces)
- **workspace.rs**: `ensure_workspace_current` now stamps the binary's `WAI_GIT_COMMIT` into config alongside the version, triggered when either value differs
- **init.rs**: New workspaces are seeded with `tool_commit` from the start
- **doctor.rs**: `check_version` now uses commit hash as primary staleness signal:
  1. Empty `tool_commit` → warns workspace not yet synced to commit tracking
  2. Commit mismatch → warns with both hashes so user can see what changed
  3. Commit matches → passes (shows version + commit)
  Falls back to version-string comparison when binary has no git info

## Result

Doctor now detects workspace staleness on every commit, without any manual version bumping required.
title: Add commit-based workspace staleness detection to doctor

description:
Scaffold a new skill directory in `.wai/resources/agent-config/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`.

## Behaviour
1. Validate name via `validate_skill_name()`
2. Check directory doesn't already exist (error if it does)
3. Create `<name>/SKILL.md` with frontmatter template:
   ```
   ---
   name: <name>
   description: ""
   ---

   # <Title Case Name>

   Instructions go here.
   ```
4. Print success + remind to run `wai sync`
5. Respect `--safe` mode (refuse if set)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Creates correct directory structure under agent-config/skills/
- Template has valid YAML frontmatter
- Title case conversion for heading (e.g., my-skill → My Skill)
- Error on duplicate name
- Safe mode respected

description:
Tool work: new CLI subcommand in src/. Requires openspec proposal before implementation (openspec/AGENTS.md). This ticket covers the design stage — implementation is a follow-on ticket.

From user behavior research: session end neglect — many threads end with 'commit and push' with no handoff. Design a 'wai close' CLI subcommand that wraps 'wai handoff create <project>' + prints 'bd sync --from-main' reminder + shows git status. Automates the session-close checklist into one command.

This is the long-term replacement for checklist-based approaches (CLAUDE.md already has the checklist at L65, but it isn't consistently triggered — the trigger is the gap).

Target behavior:
  $ wai close
  ✓ Handoff created: .wai/projects/why-command/handoffs/2026-02-24-session.md
  ! Uncommitted changes: src/main.rs, CLAUDE.md
  → Next: bd sync --from-main && git add <files> && git commit

Note: handoff-system spec marks automatic generation as non-goal. Hook-based triggering (user still invokes wai close) is different and compatible with the spec. Part of planned wai-ux project.

See: .wai/projects/why-command/research/2026-02-24-user-behavior-analysis-claude-amp-gemini-ope.md Gap B / Priority 4.
notes: Openspec proposal scaffolded: openspec/changes/add-close-command/. Validated. Ready for approval before implementation.
title: Design wai close: session end helper command

description:
Tool work: implement the `wai prime` top-level CLI command per openspec/changes/add-prime-command/. Requires approved openspec before merging.

## What
New `wai prime` command that gives a single at-a-glance orientation: active project, phase, last handoff summary, plugin status summaries, and suggested next action.

Expected output:
```
◆ wai prime — 2026-02-24
• Project: why-command [review]
• Handoff: 2026-02-23 — 'Completed Phase 9.1 verbosity levels...'
• Beads:   3 open issues (2 ready)
• Spec:    add-why-command: 49/54 (91%)
→ Suggested next: bd show wai-abc
```

## Key design decisions (see openspec/changes/add-prime-command/design.md)
- Plugin summaries: call `plugin::run_hooks(project_root, "on_status")` (src/commands/status.rs:89) and `openspec::read_status(project_root)` (src/commands/status.rs:92) — do NOT duplicate or shell out to wai status; re-verify line numbers at start of implementation (task 3.0)
- Suggested next: invoke `bd ready --json`, use first element's `id` field; skip silently on any error
- Handoff snippet: graceful degradation on missing/malformed frontmatter; "no summary yet" when handoff has no paragraph content

- [ ] 3.1 Implement Claude API client (reqwest-based, optional)

## Architecture

LLMClient trait:
  fn complete(&self, prompt: &str) -> Result<String>
  fn is_available(&self) -> bool

Implementors: ClaudeClient (API via reqwest), OllamaClient (process spawn).

Backend selection priority:
1. Explicit config [why] llm = "..." takes precedence
2. Auto-detect: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + responds → Claude; ollama binary + model available → Ollama
3. Fall back to wai search if none available

Prompt template structure: role (oracle), user question, project context (phase + commits), available artifacts, git context (file queries only), task instructions (find 3-5 relevant artifacts, synthesize narrative).

description:
Tool work: new CLI subcommand or flag in src/. Requires openspec proposal before implementation (openspec/AGENTS.md). This ticket covers the design stage — resolve the design questions below before writing any Rust.

From user behavior research: wai status is per-repo. No way to see all projects using wai and their phase/open issues at a glance. Design 'wai ls' (or 'wai status --global' flag) that scans for .wai/ directories and reports phase + open issue count per project.

Key design questions to resolve before implementation:
- Scan strategy: walk upward from cwd? Scan a configured list of roots in ~/.wai/config.toml? Both?
- Performance: what if there are 50+ repos? Should scan be lazy/cached?
- Output: one line per repo or a structured table?
- Scope: only repos the current user owns, or any .wai/ found on path?

Suggested output sketch:
  why-command   [review]   3 open, 2 ready
  para          [plan]     7 open, 0 ready
  rizomas       [implement] 1 open, 1 ready

Genuine new capability with no existing equivalent.

See: .wai/projects/why-command/research/2026-02-24-user-behavior-analysis-claude-amp-gemini-ope.md Gap E / Priority 6.

description:
Add `check_test_coverage(repo_root)` to `wai way` (`src/commands/way.rs`).

## Goal
Encourage projects to configure a coverage tool *with an enforced threshold*, nudging teams toward 100% or a near-100% target. The check is static (filesystem only — no subprocess, no test run).

## check_test_coverage(repo_root)
- **Name**: "Test coverage"
- **Intent**: Enforced coverage thresholds catch regressions automatically and keep quality high.
- **Success criteria**: A coverage tool is configured with a minimum threshold.

### Detection logic (check in order, stop on first match)

"Stop on first match" is intentional and matches the existing `way.rs` pattern (see `check_task_runner`). It targets the primary language of the repo — not a full multi-language audit.

---

**Rust — tarpaulin**
- Config files: `tarpaulin.toml` at repo root (no dotfile variant), or `[package.metadata.tarpaulin]` / `[workspace.metadata.tarpaulin]` section in `Cargo.toml`
- Secondary: check `Cargo.toml` dev-dependencies for `cargo-llvm-cov` as an alternative Rust coverage tool
- Threshold present: config contains `fail-under`
- Pass message (threshold found): "tarpaulin configured (threshold enforced)"
- Pass message (no threshold): "tarpaulin configured"
- Suggestion when threshold missing: "Add `fail-under` to tarpaulin config to enforce a minimum — https://github.com/xd009642/tarpaulin"
- For cargo-llvm-cov detection: Pass message "cargo-llvm-cov detected", suggestion "Configure a threshold via CI flags or llvm-cov config — https://github.com/taiki-e/cargo-llvm-cov"

**Python — coverage.py / pytest-cov**
- Config: `[tool.coverage.report]` section in `pyproject.toml`, or `.coveragerc` at repo root
- Threshold present: config contains `fail_under`
- Pass message (threshold found): "coverage.py configured (threshold enforced)"
- Pass message (no threshold): "coverage.py configured"
- Suggestion when threshold missing: "Add `fail_under` to [tool.coverage.report] in pyproject.toml — https://coverage.readthedocs.io"

**JavaScript / TypeScript — vitest**
- Config: `vitest.config.ts`, `vitest.config.js`, or `vitest.config.mts` at repo root and file contains "coverage"
- Threshold present: file contains "thresholds"
- Pass message (threshold found): "vitest coverage configured (threshold enforced)"
- Pass message (no threshold): "vitest coverage configured"
- Suggestion when threshold missing: "Add `thresholds` to the coverage block in vitest.config — https://vitest.dev/config/#coverage"

**JavaScript / TypeScript — nyc or c8**
- Config: `nyc` or `c8` key in `package.json`, or `.nycrc` / `.nycrc.json` / `c8.config.js` at repo root
- Threshold present: config contains `branches` or `lines` (string-contains is acceptable; matches existing codebase pattern — known minor false-positive risk in comments, acceptable)
- Pass message (threshold found): "nyc/c8 configured (threshold enforced)"
- Pass message (no threshold): "nyc/c8 configured"
- Suggestion when threshold missing: "Add branch/line thresholds to nyc or c8 config — https://github.com/istanbuljs/nyc · https://github.com/bcoe/c8"

**Any language — codecov / coveralls**
- Config: `.codecov.yml` / `codecov.yml` at repo root, or `.coveralls.yml`
- Pass message: "Coverage reporting service detected (codecov/coveralls)"
- Suggestion: **always show** "Add a coverage threshold in the config to enforce minimums" (codecov/coveralls configs rarely embed thresholds directly; the nudge is always relevant)

**Nothing detected**
- Status::Info, message: "No coverage tool configured"
- Suggestion: "Configure a coverage tool with an enforced threshold — Rust: https://github.com/xd009642/tarpaulin · Python: https://coverage.readthedocs.io · JS: https://github.com/istanbuljs/nyc"

---

### Status rules
- Pass: coverage tool found (regardless of threshold)
- Pass + suggestion: tool found but no threshold — nudge to add one (or always for codecov/coveralls)
- Info: no coverage tool at all

## Integration
Add `check_test_coverage(&repo_root)` to the `checks` vec in `run()` in `src/commands/way.rs`, alongside existing checks.

## Notes
- Pure filesystem reads only — no subprocess calls, no test execution
- Check repo_root directly (no recursive search)
- Use `.exists() && path.is_dir()` for directory checks; `.exists()` for files
- Use `std::fs::read_to_string().ok()` + `.contains()` for threshold detection — same pattern as `has_cargo_dist_in_toml` in way.rs. No full TOML/JSON parse needed.
- Add tests in `tests/integration.rs` covering:
  - tarpaulin.toml present, fail-under present → Pass, no suggestion
  - tarpaulin.toml present, no fail-under → Pass + suggestion
  - nothing configured → Info
  - vitest.config.ts with coverage + thresholds → Pass, no suggestion
  Model on existing way check tests.
title: feat(way): add test coverage check
